title: Weekly client sync and pilot readiness review
expected_decisions: Meeting seventeen will be the current best backup demo example.
The ten-minute realistic recording will remain the main proof of quality.
The thirty-minute recording will be used as a stress test rather than the default live demo.
Logging visibility for processing-stage durations will be improved in a later pass.
The product will use practical positioning rather than a broad platform pitch.
The first pilot audience will be consultants, agencies, and small startup teams.

expected_actions.action: Keep one backup meeting processed and ready before any live demo.
Create the clean ten-minute audio test and run it through the product.
Review and finalize the landing page and outreach message.
Add stage timing logs to the worker output.
Package the final demo commands into one short runbook.

expected_risks: Longer recordings may take significant processing time and may exceed the current timeout.
Processing a meeting before its raw media path is attached can cause a worker error.
Restarting services during an active job can interrupt processing and create uncertainty about job state.
resolved_questions: Whether to rely only on a processed backup or also demonstrate a live upload was resolved by planning to show the backup first and upload a shorter file live if time allows.
expected_quality_concerns: Some key points may remain repetitive because the model preserves meeting-framing language.

acceptable_additional_actions.action: Save the strongest current output as the primary demo artifact and retain the older example only for internal comparison.
Keep a short command checklist for meeting creation, upload, logs, JSON notes, and Markdown notes.
Keep the current action-item cleanup and defer deeper summarization tuning until after the pilot.

must_not_invent: Separate duplicate decisions from repeated transcript sections
Separate duplicate actions from repeated transcript sections
Deadlines for actions other than today and Friday
The thirty-minute recording as the default live demonstration
An unresolved question about the demo approach
